purpose: Public-source stakeholder landscape for a hypothetical global AI policy function.
disclosure: Portfolio sample only. No relationship, contact, endorsement, or scheduled engagement is claimed.
operating_note: All owners, next actions, and dates are hypothetical internal planning fields. No outreach is authorized by this file.
data_handling_standard: Public organizational information only. Do not add private contact details, inferred personal data, or interaction history without a lawful purpose and evidence.

stakeholders.organization: NIST Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI)
stakeholders.relevance: Primary federal standards channel for secure and interoperable agents.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: CAISI agent standards and technical-program staff; identify a named public official only for a real, authorized engagement.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: NIST describes industry-led standards, open protocols, security, identity, authorization, interoperability, and evaluation as initiative pillars.
stakeholders.next_question: Which agent identity and authorization use cases are mature enough for near-term conformance work?
stakeholders.next_action: Monitor new official outputs; prepare a non-confidential evidence matrix only.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: No personal contact data or non-public interaction record stored.

stakeholders.organization: U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security
stakeholders.relevance: Compute availability, customer screening, licensing, and re-export risk.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: BIS export-administration policy or outreach staff; route substantive licensing questions through qualified export counsel.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: BIS administers and enforces U.S. export controls, including advanced-computing rules and guidance.
stakeholders.next_question: Which ownership, end-use, and product changes should trigger a refreshed licensing analysis?
stakeholders.next_action: Monitor EAR and official guidance; maintain an internal trigger matrix without seeking informal legal clearance.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: No personal contact data or licensing case information stored.

stakeholders.relevance: Central implementation and supervision channel for EU AI rules.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: AI Office policy or implementation staff relevant to transparency and standards.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: The AI Office supports EU AI Act implementation, codes, guidance, and coordination within the Commission.
stakeholders.next_question: What implementation evidence will make Article 50 disclosure and marking solutions credible?
stakeholders.next_action: Track Article 50 guidance, code updates, signatory materials, and official implementation events.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public institutional materials only; no individual profile maintained.

stakeholders.relevance: Practical, pro-innovation governance and testing route for agent deployments.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: AI governance, assurance, or sandbox program staff.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: IMDA promotes practical governance, assurance, and experimentation, including a 2026 framework and sandbox insights for agentic AI.
stakeholders.next_question: Which evidence from enterprise agent deployments would be most useful to future sandbox and framework work?
stakeholders.next_action: Complete a paper crosswalk and monitor for an official participation route; do not imply sandbox participation.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public program information only; no application or participant data stored.

stakeholders.relevance: Key APAC implementation and industrial-policy stakeholder.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: AI policy, implementation-guidance, or international-cooperation staff; Korean-language ownership required.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: MSIT is implementing Korea’s AI Basic Act while supporting AI industry development, safety, transparency, and infrastructure.
stakeholders.next_question: How will final guidance distinguish generative AI, high-impact AI, and ordinary enterprise tools in practice?
stakeholders.next_action: Assign a Korean-language monitor and validate new notices before product classification claims.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public ministry sources only; no personal contact data stored.

stakeholders.relevance: Existing-law and market-development lens for consumer-facing agents.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: Consumer-protection and digital-markets policy staff relevant to agentic AI.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: The CMA’s 2026 publication examines agentic-AI opportunities, consumer risks, applicable law, and steps businesses can take.
stakeholders.next_question: Which disclosure, cancellation, redress, and conflict controls best preserve consumer agency?
stakeholders.next_action: Monitor official updates and test public recommendations against a consumer-agent journey.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public regulator information only; no consumer or case data stored.

stakeholders.relevance: Bridge between governance guidance and practical evaluation evidence.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: AI assurance, testing-framework, or ecosystem program staff.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: The Foundation develops practical testing and governance resources for trustworthy AI and publishes crosswalks and ecosystem materials.
stakeholders.next_question: Which agent-control claims can be tested consistently across products and vendors?
stakeholders.next_action: Map agent-specific evidence gaps without claiming an assessment or foundation participation.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public foundation information only; no member or participant data stored.

stakeholders.relevance: Comparative policy evidence and international terminology alignment.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: OECD.AI provides comparative policy, measurement, and international-cooperation resources.
stakeholders.next_question: How should agentic-AI policy measures be classified for comparable cross-country monitoring?
stakeholders.next_action: Review terminology and database updates for agent-related classifications.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public institutional information only; no personal contact data stored.

stakeholders.relevance: Industry coordination across AI, cloud, semiconductors, and international policy.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: AI policy program staff; membership or coalition access is not assumed.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: ITI publishes technology-industry AI policy recommendations and public materials across accountability, transparency, standards, trade, and adoption.
stakeholders.next_question: Where is cross-industry alignment strongest on agent accountability and interoperability?
stakeholders.next_action: Track public positions and compare them with regulator, standards, and civil-society views.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public association materials only; no member or personal contact data stored.

stakeholders.organization: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence
stakeholders.relevance: Academic evidence and convening at the technology-policy intersection.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: Public policy research or convening staff; select a named researcher only when their published work is directly relevant.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: Stanford HAI publishes research and policy work on human-centered AI, governance, measurement, and societal impacts.
stakeholders.next_question: Which empirical methods can test whether agent governance controls change real-world outcomes?
stakeholders.next_action: Monitor relevant public research and record methods that could improve calibration or evaluation.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public institutional and publication data only; no inferred researcher profile.

stakeholders.relevance: Rights, contestability, and public-interest perspective on automated systems.
stakeholders.public_counterpart_role: AI Governance Lab or public-interest policy staff relevant to accountability and rights.
stakeholders.public_stance_summary: CDT publishes technically informed work on AI accountability, auditing, privacy, civil liberties, and public-interest participation.
stakeholders.next_question: What transparency and redress are needed when agents act for, transact for, or influence people?
stakeholders.next_action: Use public materials to test contestability, accessibility, privacy, and redress assumptions; no outreach authorized.
stakeholders.data_handling_note: Public organizational information only; no personal contact data stored.
